reasoning.A:
DECISION: Pass  
REASONING: I’m fully confident my own answer to Q1 is correct (45 ÷ 5 is straightforward), and historically Q2–Q4 have almost always been right in these simple arithmetic games. In all recent games, someone else (usually B or C) has volunteered in Round 1, so the probability that *no one* volunteers—and we face a random kill—is low.  

If I volunteer, I personally take on the full risk that any of Q2–Q4 might be wrong. If I pass, my death risk is only 25% of the already small chance that nobody volunteers. Verifying would just cost 1 coin and doesn’t reduce the random-death risk this round, so passing best preserves both my survival odds and my coins.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My puzzle 24 + 63 is simple and I’m fully confident in my sealed answer. Every recent game has used straightforward arithmetic, every verification has matched existing capsules, and the single-capsule set Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D has consistently produced a correct password. The chance that one of the others made an arithmetic error seems much smaller than the 25% random-death risk if nobody volunteers. Verifying first would cost a coin and still expose me to random elimination this round, so volunteering now with the only available capsule for each question maximizes my survival chances while preserving all 4 coins if the password is correct.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I know my own answer for Q3 (12 × 5) is correct. Across many games, every recent capsule has been correct on the first attempt, with only one early mistake ever observed from D and none since. There are no conflicting capsules now, so Q1-A, Q2-B, and Q4-D are each very likely correct.  

If I don’t volunteer, I still face the risk of random death this round if others also wait, and paying a coin to verify doesn’t reduce that immediate risk—it only makes me poorer next game. Given the strong accuracy track record and the simplicity of the arithmetic, the probability that the full password using Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D is correct is higher than my effective risk from random elimination, so volunteering maximizes my chance of surviving and keeping all 4 coins.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
✅ C entered CORRECT password using capsules: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D
